title: ASTS Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript Summary
text: In the Q1 2026 earnings call, AST SpaceMobile management provided updates on satellite deployment progress, operational challenges, and strategic partnerships. The company reaffirmed its 2026 revenue guidance of $150-200 million and highlighted growing contracted revenue commitments exceeding $1.2 billion. Management discussed the BlueBird 7 satellite loss and its implications for the launch schedule, while noting that remaining satellites are ready for deployment. The company emphasized strong partnerships with Vodafone, Verizon, AT&T, and government agencies providing revenue predictability. Operating expense guidance of $70-80 million per quarter and capital expenditure of $350-425 million per quarter was reiterated, reflecting the company's capital-intensive phase. Management expressed confidence in achieving the 45-satellite deployment target by end-of-2026 despite recent setbacks. The discussion highlighted the company's focus on manufacturing scale-up with new facilities in Texas and Florida, domestic supply chain integration, and government contract execution.
